017package org.apache.juneau.rest.client.jetty;
018
019import java.io.*;
020import java.util.concurrent.*;
021
022import org.apache.juneau.rest.client.*;
023import org.eclipse.jetty.client.*;
024
025/**
026 * {@link HttpTransport} implementation backed by Jetty 12 {@link HttpClient}.
027 *
028 * <p>
029 * This transport is auto-discovered via {@link java.util.ServiceLoader} when
030 * {@code org.eclipse.jetty:jetty-client} is on the classpath.  You can also instantiate it explicitly:
031 *
032 * <p class='bjava'>
033 *    <jv>transport</jv> = JettyHttpTransport.<jsm>builder</jsm>()
034 *       .httpClient(<jk>new</jk> HttpClient())
035 *       .build();
036 *
037 *    <jv>client</jv> = RestClient.<jsm>builder</jsm>()
038 *       .transport(<jv>transport</jv>)
039 *       .build();
040 * </p>
041 *
042 * <p>
043 * The Jetty {@link HttpClient} is started automatically when the transport is created and stopped when
044 * {@link #close()} is called.  The request body is streamed via {@link OutputStreamRequestContent} using
045 * a background thread, avoiding full in-memory buffering.  The response header timeout defaults to 30 seconds
046 * and is configurable via {@link JettyHttpTransportBuilder#responseTimeoutMs(long)}.
047 *
048 * <p>
049 * <b>Beta — API subject to change:</b> This type is part of the next-generation REST client and HTTP stack
050 * ({@code org.apache.juneau.marshall.ng.*}).
051 * It is not API-frozen: binary- and source-incompatible changes may appear in the <b>next major</b> Juneau release
052 * (and possibly earlier).
053 *
054 * <h5 class='section'>See Also:</h5><ul>
055 *    <li class='link'><a class="doclink" href="https://juneau.apache.org/docs/topics/NextGenRestClient">juneau-ng REST client</a>
056 * </ul>
057 *
058 * @since 9.2.1
059 */
060@SuppressWarnings({
061   "resource" // Transport methods return Closeables owned by the caller; Eclipse JDT @Owning warning is by design.
062})
063public final class JettyHttpTransport implements HttpTransport {
064
065   private final HttpClient httpClient;
066   private final long responseTimeoutMs;
067
068   JettyHttpTransport(JettyHttpTransportBuilder builder) throws Exception {
069      this.httpClient = builder.httpClient != null ? builder.httpClient : new HttpClient();
070      this.responseTimeoutMs = builder.responseTimeoutMs;
071      if (!this.httpClient.isStarted())
072         this.httpClient.start();
073   }
074
075   /**
076    * Returns a new builder for this transport.
077    *
078    * @return A new builder. Never <jk>null</jk>.
079    */
080   public static JettyHttpTransportBuilder builder() {
081      return new JettyHttpTransportBuilder();
082   }
083
084   /**
085    * Returns a new instance backed by a default {@link HttpClient}.
086    *
087    * @return A new instance. Never <jk>null</jk>.
088    * @throws Exception If the Jetty {@link HttpClient} cannot be started.
089    */
090   public static JettyHttpTransport create() throws Exception {
091      return builder().build();
092   }
093
094   @Override /* HttpTransport */
095   public TransportResponse execute(TransportRequest request) throws TransportException {
096      var jettyRequest = buildJettyRequest(request);
097      var listener = new InputStreamResponseListener();
098      jettyRequest.send(listener);
099      Response jettyResponse;
100      try {
101         jettyResponse = responseTimeoutMs > 0
102            ? listener.get(responseTimeoutMs, TimeUnit.MILLISECONDS)
103            : listener.get(Long.MAX_VALUE, TimeUnit.MILLISECONDS);
104      } catch (InterruptedException e) {
105         Thread.currentThread().interrupt();
106         throw new TransportException("HTTP request interrupted", e);
107      } catch (TimeoutException e) {
108         throw new TransportException("HTTP request timed out", e);
109      } catch (ExecutionException e) {
110         throw new TransportException("HTTP transport error: " + e.getCause().getMessage(), e.getCause());
111      }
112      return buildTransportResponse(jettyResponse, listener.getInputStream());
113   }
114
115   @Override /* Closeable */
116   public void close() throws IOException {
117      try {
118         httpClient.stop();
119      } catch (Exception e) {
120         throw new IOException("Failed to stop Jetty HttpClient: " + e.getMessage(), e);
121      }
122   }
123
124   // -----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
125   // Internal helpers
126   // -----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
127
128   private Request buildJettyRequest(TransportRequest request) {
129      var jettyRequest = httpClient.newRequest(request.getUri()).method(request.getMethod());
130      for (var h : request.getHeaders())
131         jettyRequest.headers(fields -> fields.add(h.name(), h.value()));
132      var body = request.getBody();
133      if (body != null)
134         jettyRequest.body(buildRequestContent(body));
135      return jettyRequest;
136   }
137
138   private static OutputStreamRequestContent buildRequestContent(TransportBody body) {
139      var ct = body.getContentType();
140      var contentType = ct != null ? ct : "application/octet-stream";
141      var content = new OutputStreamRequestContent(contentType);
142      // Write body to the content's OutputStream on a background thread so Jetty can stream it concurrently.
143      var writer = new Thread(() -> {
144         try (var out = content.getOutputStream()) {
145            body.writeTo(out);
146         } catch (IOException e) {
147            // Closing the stream on error signals Jetty to abort the request.
148         }
149      }, "juneau-ng-body-writer");
150      writer.setDaemon(true);
151      writer.start();
152      return content;
153   }
154
155   private static TransportResponse buildTransportResponse(Response jettyResponse, InputStream bodyStream) {
156      var builder = TransportResponse.builder()
157         .statusCode(jettyResponse.getStatus())
158         .reasonPhrase(jettyResponse.getReason())
159         .body(bodyStream);
160      jettyResponse.getHeaders().forEach(field -> builder.header(field.getName(), field.getValue()));
161      return builder.build();
162   }
163}
